channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: We need to put human creativity at the center of adtech
item.description: I’ve been searching for the words to describe my feelings towards the current state of adtech. Terms like “stale,” “stagnant,” and “boring” are among the pejoratives that come to mind.
item.dc:creator: Chris Gadek, Fast Company

item.title: Smile! You can now control your Chromebook with your face
item.description: Google has started rolling out a number of new features in Chrome OS, according to The Verge, including a new feature that allows a user to control their Chromebook using head movements (to move the mouse pointer) and facial expressions (to click or activate dictation).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fake Reddit sites found pushing Lumma Stealer malware
item.description: Hackers are faking Reddit threads and WeTransfer files to try and trick users into downloading malware.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia RTX 5080 Geekbench Results Leaked: What They Mean for PC Gamers
item.description: Nvidia's upcoming GeForce GPU, the RTX 5080, has leaked benchmark results that suggest a 20% generational increase in performance compared to the RTX 4080. However, the new card appears to come up short compared to the older RTX 4090.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: First image for Supergirl: Woman of Tomorrow shows Milly Alcock's Kara Zor-El in a place that'll be very familiar to DC comic book fans
item.description: James Gunn has revealed the first image for Supergirl: Woman of Tomorrow as filming gets underway on the DCU Chapter One movie.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Trump tasks federal agencies with drafting a new AI action plan within 180 days
item.description: President Donald Trump has signed a sweeping executive order to make the US a global leader in artificial intelligence (AI), revoking regulatory measures enacted by the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nearly 250 million children missed school last year because of extreme weather, UNICEF says
item.description: The United Nations Children's Fund says at least 242 million children in 85 countries had their schooling interrupted last year because of heatwaves, cyclones, flooding and other extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he will use energy emergency declaration to approve more power for AI
item.description: President Trump said Thursday that he will use his energy emergency declaration to approve power stations for artificial intelligence (AI) centers.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: Seeking growth, UK competition regulator probes Apple and Google
item.description: Apple and Google now face regulatory probes in the ailing UK market, where the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has confirmed it intends to investigate both company’s mobile ecosystems under new digital market laws.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Ivanka Trump calls out 'fake crypto coin' named after her
item.description: Ivanka Trump, President Trump’s eldest daughter, warned Thursday that a cryptocurrency token bearing her name is fake.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Spain's leader wants the EU to ‘make social media great again.’ Here's how
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is launching a crusade against what he sees as the downsides of social media.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NFL tells New England Patriots that team can’t have Bluesky account
item.description: The NFL directed the New England Patriots to delete the team’s Bluesky account, Fred Kirsch, the team’s VP of content, said on a January 16 episode of the Patriots Unfiltered podcast.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI says India court can’t hear case on alleged copyright breach
item.description: OpenAI has told an Indian court that any order to remove training data powering its ChatGPT service would be inconsistent with its legal obligations in the United States, according to a recent filing seen by Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Trump rescinds Biden’s executive order on AI safety, as promised
item.description: Hours after returning to the White House, President Donald Trump made a symbolic mark on the future of artificial intelligence by repealing former President Joe Biden’s guardrails for the fast-developing technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Musk clashes with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over Trump-supported Stargate AI data center project
item.description: Elon Musk is clashing with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over the Stargate artificial intelligence infrastructure project touted by President Donald Trump, the latest in a feud between the two tech billionaires that started on OpenAI's board and is now testing Musk's influence with the new president.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cisco CEO Robbins on AI: Pressure to deploy is real
item.description: Interest in AI has exploded over the last two or three years, but enterprises are only just beginning to think about how they’re going to take advantage of it, Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins told the audience at the company’s recent AI Summit.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Amazon to close 7 warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec and eliminate 1,700 jobs
item.description: Online retailer Amazon said Wednesday that it's closing all seven of its warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec in the next two months. The e-commerce giant said the move would provide “even more savings to our customers over the long run.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Stratoshark brings Wireshark-style analysis to cloud system calls
item.description: The open-source Wireshark network protocol analyzer has been a standard tool for networking professionals for decades. In recent years, organizations have increasingly moved workloads to the cloud, where they have not had the same network visibility.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stargate Project launched for OpenAI AI infrastructure
item.description: In a venture unveiled by Pres. Donald Trump on January 21, companies including SoftBank, Oracle, OpenAI, and MGX are funding a new AI company, called the Stargate Project, to invest $500 billion to build AI infrastructure for OpenAI in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A new security seal of approval is coming to your smart home gadgets
item.description: The White House launched a new cybersecurity safety label, the U.S. Cyber Trust Mark, intended to help consumers make informed decisions on smart device safety.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to deal with a Big Pile of Mud
item.description: Last week I talked about where those big, unwieldy code bases come from. They don’t appear out of nowhere, and no one ever plans to create one.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump pardons founder of Silk Road website
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced Tuesday that he had pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump open to Elon Musk buying TikTok
item.description: TikTok restored service to U.S. users Sunday afternoon and credited Trump for pushing to keep the app operational.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: AI experiment in halfpipe judging at X Games will give snowboarders a glimpse into the future
item.description: The X Games will experiment judging halfpipe runs this week in Aspen using artificial intelligence, the cutting-edge technology that could someday play a role in the way subjectively judged sports are scored.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sustainability, grid demands, AI workloads will challenge data center growth in 2025
item.description: Data centers this year will face several challenges as the demand for artificial intelligence introduces an evolution in AI hardware, on-premises and cloud-based strategies for training and inference, and innovations in power distributions—all while opposition to new data center developments continu
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated — but that's likely because they had previously followed official U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Red Hat unveils Kubernetes connectivity solution to tame multi-cloud chaos
item.description: Integrating traffic management, policy enforcement, and role-based access control, Red Hat Connectivity Link is a new technology from IBM’s Red Hat business unit that’s aimed at simplifying how enterprises manage application connectivity across distributed cloud environments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Sony’s surprising entry into electric vehicle market
item.description: Sony and Honda's Afeela 1 EV launches with advanced tech and entertainment for $89,900. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives his takeaways.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The bitter lesson for generative AI adoption
item.description: The rapid development and improvement in generative AI technology pose a challenge for training and fine-tuning as a sustainable path for adoption. If organizations constantly need to fine-tune new models for specific tasks, they might be in a costly cycle of catching up with new technology.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump, a populist president, is flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Some of the most exclusive seats at President Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday were reserved for powerful tech CEOs who also happen to be among the world’s richest men.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
